zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開篇論點

6月26日美國公債收盤延續本週2年至10年期債券的存續期間漲勢，但長債下跌，使此波走勢成為市場對通膨壓力緩解更具鑑別性的判斷。5年期殖利率下跌3個基點至4.12%，30年期則上升1個基點至4.87%。油價走低、家庭通膨預期降溫，以及紐約 Fed 趨勢通膨估值下降，降低了政策近期採取行動的必要性。科技股集中賣壓增添對中期美國公債的避險需求。殖利率曲線仍拒絕認可不受限制的存續期間漲勢，因為成長韌性、廣泛價格壓力及 Fed 內部分歧，使最終政策利率路徑仍不確定，而財政與買方基礎疑慮則集中在10年期以外。

因此，週五重新打通了6月24日漲勢背後的通膨降溫管道；此前一個收盤日油價反彈，曾短暫重新開啟能源尾端風險。Brent下跌4.3449%至$71.99，10年期實質殖利率與通膨補償均告下降。這項重要變化並不代表市場重返鴿派體制。Minneapolis Fed 總裁 Neel Kashkari支持今年升息1次，因為通膨已擴散至能源以外，而紐約 Fed的成長即時預測保持穩定。收盤反映市場對行動時點的迫切感下降，尤以殖利率曲線腹部為甚，但並未排除進一步抑制的可能性。這項組合解釋了為何5年期債券表現優於前端與長端，以及為何即使大多數名目殖利率下降，2s30s仍然趨陡。

信用與跨資產也對喘息和信心作出相同區分。整體股價指數幾乎持平，但市場對 AI 估值與資本支出的疑慮從亞洲股市延伸至美國時段，導致半導體股大幅下跌。SpaceX規模$250億的債券發行，相較美國公債截至週五下午已累積約$3.6億損失，顯示初級市場承載能力即使極為強勁，也不保證次級市場承接穩定。上市信用產品報酬平靜，因此壓力仍集中於長存續期間科技債及較低品質風險。主要收盤論點因而是殖利率曲線腹部的有序漲勢：能源通膨降溫與預期走軟降低了近期政策壓力，而成長韌性、Fed的警戒態度、科技企業融資需求及長端供給風險，使存續期間及美元融資借款人的出清利率維持高檔。

## 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債表現自殖利率曲線腹部以外逐步轉弱。2年期殖利率下跌2個基點至4.07%，5年期下跌3個基點至4.12%，10年期下降2個基點至4.38%。30年期上升1個基點至4.87%。全週而言，上述各期限殖利率分別下降12、11、8及3個基點。單日與單週型態均顯示，最明顯的喘息集中在受未來數個政策週期影響的期限，而長債仍保留較多對通膨持續性、財政供給及全球買方基礎不確定性的補償。

殖利率曲線指標凸顯長端阻力。2s10s利差維持在31個基點，但2s30s擴大3個基點至80。2s5s10s蝶式利差下降2個基點至-21，確認5年期相對兩側期限均表現較佳。隨著10年期相對2年期與30年期平均價格走高，2s10s30s蝶式利差下降3個基點至-18。這既不是6月24日的全面牛市趨平，也不是首次 FOMC 後賣壓之後單純由前端帶動的牛市趨陡，而是以政策與成長交會處為核心的漲勢，較遠期限則繼續承擔尚未消除的期限溢價。

通膨連結定價顯示，10年期名目債券漲勢的構成相當平均。10年期實質殖利率下降1個基點至2.18%，10年期損益兩平通膨率也下降1個基點至2.20%。5年期與7年期實質殖利率分別下降1及2個基點至1.91%及2.03%，30年期實質殖利率則維持在2.69%不變。隨著近期升息焦慮消退，投資人接受殖利率曲線腹部略低的實質補償，卻未放寬最長端的實質利率門檻。損益兩平通膨率下降顯示，在前一交易日逆轉後，能源價格走低再次進入名目定價。

油價為這項變化提供最明確的理由。WTI下跌3.7403%至$69.23，單週跌幅達9.6214%，Brent單週跌幅則逼近10%。荷姆茲海峽的油輪運輸持續，降低了週四船隻遇襲會重新造成持久實體短缺的機率。此走勢從通膨補償中移除部分近期燃料與運費衝擊。這並未解決更廣泛的價格持續性問題，因為 Fed 偏好的通膨指標截至5月持續加速，且數位官員認為價格壓力已擴散至多個類別。

家庭預期與模型估算的趨勢通膨仍降低了近期預期失去錨定的風險。密西根大學調查顯示，5年至10年期通膨預期為3.3%，低於先前的3.4%。紐約 Fed的Multivariate Core Trend估值從4月向下修正後的3.7%降至5月的3.4%，其68%機率區間為3.0%至3.9%。住房以外服務仍是高於趨勢的最大貢獻來源，但超額貢獻由0.91個百分點降至0.83個百分點。商品與住房的貢獻也有所緩和，使改善範圍超越單一能源項目。

成長證據阻止通膨壓力緩解演變為寬鬆敘事。紐約聯準銀行 Staff Nowcast估算第2季實質 GDP 成長2.71%，與1週前的2.68%變化不大。其區間寬廣，意味精確度應審慎看待，但中央估值並未顯示需求突然斷裂。Fed 會議後受訪的經濟學家也將第4季核心 PCE 預測上調至3.2%，並提高新增就業預估，其中位數路徑顯示 Fed 將維持利率不變至6月 2027。這項組合較支持延長暫停升息，而非即將降息，使利率持續對通膨廣度與勞動市場韌性敏感。

Fed 溝通強化了這項不對稱性。Kashkari於週五表示，廣泛通膨使他在今年的預測中納入1次升息。6月會議後，19位決策者中有9位預測至少升息1次。市場定價仍轉趨溫和：截至週五，交易員認為年底前升息2次的機率約為25%，低於週一近80%。這次重新定價降低的是快速連續升息的機率，而非採取抑制措施的可能性。這與2年期殖利率下降、殖利率曲線腹部價格走高，以及長端實質殖利率不變一致。

現金國庫券殖利率曲線在收盤時未顯示一致的季末融資干擾。4週期票息等值殖利率維持在3.68%不變，6週期下降2個基點至3.67%，8週期下降1個基點至3.73%。13週期下降3個基點至3.75%，17週期與26週期均下降1個基點至3.85%及3.93%。52週期殖利率下降3個基點至3.93%。局部差異依舊存在，特別是13週至52週區段相較1個月前的升幅，但隨市場接近7月2日的大額交割日，週五整體方向偏強。

紐約收盤後的週末評論延續了下一交易日的政策限制，但不能用來解釋週五的走勢。Richmond Fed 總裁 Tom Barkin於週日表示，通膨過高，但汽油價格下跌及消費者抗拒漲價帶來初步緩解。他也將部分價格壓力與 AI 基礎設施連結，並認為溫和限制性的立場合理。他對消費韌性與企業定價的強調呼應收盤時對長端的審慎看法：能源價格可以迅速降溫，但服務、薪酬與資本密集型投資將決定通膨降溫能否持續。

因此，利率結果延續本週漲勢，同時再次改變其內部訊息。6月24日時，能源壓力緩解擴散至實質殖利率下降；6月25日原油反彈則重新帶回損益兩平通膨率風險。週五恢復2項成分同步下降，5年期區段最為受惠。30年期債券遭到賣壓、成長即時預測穩定及 Fed 內部鷹派少數，使這項改善未成為限制性政策已經結束的定論。殖利率曲線腹部最能兼顧衝擊型通膨消退與未來成長放緩風險；長端則仍是財政、供給及持續性不確定性的承載處。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

整體上市信用產品報酬平淡。LQD維持在$109.50不變，HYG與JNK均下跌約0.06%。其單週變動仍然有限：LQD上漲0.3942%，HYG下跌0.2250%，JNK下跌0.1764%。整體走勢既未顯示全面融資凍結，也未出現對較低品質風險的強勁追逐。美國公債上漲支撐較高評級存續期間，而科技股賣壓與仍高的實質利率結構，使投機級債券投資人保持審慎。

SpaceX直接顯示發行後承壓的證據。交易商報價顯示，該公司2056年到期債券相較發行時高於美國公債175個基點的利差，最多擴大28個基點。最長期限債券抹去承銷配售期間的利差收窄幅度，此前需求曾接近$900億。規模$250億的發行案相較美國公債的損失，從週四 late約$3.05億增至週五下午約$3.6億。5年期債券吸引最強需求，20年期及30年期券種的需求降幅與隨後弱勢則最大。

這項惡化改變了本週成功配售的意義。投資人展現了為超大型投資級借款人提供資金的能力，但次級市場顯示，訂單簿可能有相當部分依賴快錢需求及立即取得交易收益的預期。預計多年出現負現金流、治理結構集中及交易規模擴大，使長期風險難以形成穩定價格。SpaceX股票保持穩定也未能修復債券，因為債權人的上行收益固定，卻要承擔數十年的資本支出與技術淘汰風險。

科技信用市場呈現這類壓力更廣泛但較溫和的版本。截至週四，美國高評級科技債風險溢價由5月底的0.74個百分點擴大至0.79個百分點。Alphabet宣布規模$850億的股票發行後，其債券走軟，儘管新股發行通常會增加債權人的保障墊。市場將增資部分解讀為 AI 支出與未來借款可能高於原先假設的訊號。股票發行改善了近期流動性，同時也揭示此輪融資週期的規模。

供給背景解釋了這種敏感度。截至週二，美國高評級債券發行已達$1,750億，創下6月紀錄。週末引用的估計顯示，截至2030年的 AI 與資料中心支出為$5.5兆，未來5年潛在高評級資料中心融資則達$2.1兆。這些預測並非已完成發行，但足以說明買方為何要求按期限與發行人進行更明確的區分。資本密集度可讓市場准入維持暢通，卻也會逐步提高市場承接每一美元新增長存續期間科技債所要求的利差。

約翰尼斯堡展現另一種優先保障債權人的形式。該市償還14.4億蘭特、即$8,800萬、票息11.455%的債券，完成贖回其300億蘭特國內中期票據計畫。即使財政部長因具爭議性的103億蘭特薪資協議威脅扣留80億蘭特中央撥款，該筆付款仍保障了債券持有人。因此，計畫中的未來基礎設施債券，將在成功償還到期債務、但營運及政府間財政壓力嚴峻的背景下進入市場。

信用市場收盤整體流動性充足，但會懲罰存續期間、槓桿或治理結構造成估值不確定的標的。上市基金報酬平靜無法抵銷SpaceX利差直接擴大，最初的訂單簿也未確立借款人的長期出清成本。科技企業仍能以極大規模籌集債務與股權資金，約翰尼斯堡也在財政承壓下履行合約付款。共同主軸是取得融資後伴隨更嚴格的價格發現，最長期且結構最弱的債權承擔最大調整。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

歐洲利率同樣受惠於能源壓力緩解，但仍保有政策底部。交易員將截至2026年底定價的 ECB 額外緊縮幅度降至不足25個基點，2週前市場尚反映完整1次升息。存款利率為2.25%。德國10年期殖利率降至2.85%，為3月10日以來最低，歐元區2年期通膨交換由4月超過3%回落至2.2%以下。油價滑落使存續期間得以回升，但並不意味通膨超標已經消失。

ECB 消費者調查釐清了這項分歧。1年期通膨預期由4.0%降至5月的3.5%，且調查時間甚至早於美國與伊朗達成協議。3年期及5年期預期仍高達2.9%及2.4%。家庭也預期未來1年產出萎縮1.7%，程度低於4月預期的2.2%降幅，而預期失業率則小幅升至11.3%。近期能源疑慮緩解，但較長期預期及家庭對成長的疲弱看法，仍使決策者必須在依然偏高的通膨路徑與脆弱需求之間取得平衡。

Isabel Schnabel於週六的發言在美國公債收盤後出現，為未來1週定下鷹派基調。她警告食品、商品及服務均有上行風險，主張能源衝擊可能擴散，並表示市場預期需要進一步升息，才能讓通膨回到2%。她也指出尚未出現薪資壓力，並承認和平協議降低了不利結果的可能性。這些較晚發表的言論並非週五德國公債或美國公債上漲的原因；它們凸顯市場隱含緊縮幅度能否下降，仍取決於各國及歐元區通膨數據。

財政資訊增添另一條存續期間傳導管道。加拿大第1季一般政府赤字由1年前占 GDP的1.9%擴大至C$164億、即2.1%。整體淨債務降至C$5,381億及 GDP的16.5%，但不含社會保障基金的債務增加4.3%至C$1.49兆。這些數字同時反映合併資產負債表比率改善、當期缺口擴大，以及不屬於有資產支應之社會保障基金的債務增加。因此，加拿大存續期間面臨的供給訊號，並不像整體淨債務下降單獨呈現的那麼溫和。

法國國家審計機關在週六的1份報告中，進一步凸顯歐洲長期供給疑慮。該機關表示，政府缺乏可信的支出路徑，無法將去年占產出5.1%的赤字降至2029年的3%。由於這項評估在美國收盤後公布，因此應納入下一次全球存續期間評估。其傳導路徑包括預期主權債發行量、法國風險溢價，以及2027年總統大選前可運用的財政空間；即使能源通膨下降，這些因素仍可能使歐洲核心及半核心市場的期限溢價維持高檔。

英國在週末增加了更直接的主權融資形式辯論。Andy Burnham聲勢上升，使透過向一般投資人發行「戰爭債券」以支應國防支出的構想再度受到討論，其中一種做法是將英國家庭每年投入 ISA 的約£700億資金，部分導向政府債券。支持者認為，具稅務優惠的國防債券可額外籌得£200億；批評者則強調，這仍是借款，並非免費的財政空間。對英國公債投資人而言，名稱不是重點：專屬零售工具或可擴大買方基礎，卻不會消除新增國防支出所帶來的債務存量、利息成本及財政規則取捨。

亞洲經濟活動並不支持全球同步放緩。新加坡5月製造業產出較1年前成長13.0%，其中電子業成長35.8%、精密工程成長32.2%，但經季節調整後總產量較前月下降0.7%。生物醫藥產出萎縮24.2%，化學品萎縮11.5%，運輸工程萎縮5.0%。狹窄的 AI 與半導體設備動能支撐區域需求與資本支出，但產業表現分散，限制了此成長訊號推高全球實質殖利率的程度。

因此，全球存續期間受惠於能源壓力共同下降，但各地面臨不同限制。歐洲近期預期下降，卻仍伴隨偏高的中期指標與鷹派 ECB 溝通。加拿大與法國使財政供給持續受到關注。新加坡展現強勁的 AI 相關產出，但並未廣泛擴及各產業。這些管道有助於解釋美國殖利率曲線的期限分化：海外通膨壓力緩解支撐美國公債殖利率曲線腹部，而財政盤算、當地主權債替代選項及持續的政策不確定性，使長端出清門檻保持較高。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場債券收盤幾乎持平。即使美元指數下跌0.0690%至101.36，EMB仍小跌0.0207%至$96.54。美元單週仍上漲0.5057%，單月上漲2.2083%，因此週五的小幅下跌並未消除 Fed 立場轉鷹所造成的外部融資限制。油價走低有利於進口國及本地通膨路徑，但依賴外資流入的國家仍容易受到美國利率及美元累積漲幅影響。

奈及利亞將複雜的負債結構轉化為即時現金。此前數週，該主權國家從與First Abu Dhabi Bank簽訂、總規模$50億的總報酬交換中提取約$15億。其以相當於借款133.3%的奈拉證券作為擔保品。第1批資金的利差為SOFR加395個基點，此後升至400個基點；資金預計用於再融資高成本債務及支應預算赤字。截至12月底，外債為$519億。

這項交換改善近期流動性，同時創造或有美元債權。Fitch警告，若國內殖利率上升或奈拉走弱，以美元支付的追繳保證金可能加劇外匯壓力；IMF也指出貨幣與匯率政策可能受到限制。安哥拉與塞內加爾曾使用類似結構。因此，奈及利亞在傳統債券發行以外取得融資，但超額擔保與跨幣別保證金機制，將部分再融資風險轉移至外匯存底、本地利率及匯率管理。

新興市場更廣泛的敏感度已由油價轉向 Fed。美國5年期殖利率與拉丁美洲同類利率的30日相關係數，由2月底的0.10升至0.49，其中與墨西哥的相關性在6月超過0.8。原油走低為石油進口國提供更多通膨空間，但美元走強使央行對釋放寬鬆訊號保持審慎。土耳其與哥倫比亞尤其突出，對外資的依賴可使美國鷹派利率路徑迅速傳導至本地殖利率及貨幣。週五的EMB小幅變動掩蓋了這項日益增強的總體連動。

塞內加爾顯示，個別國家的財政可信度足以蓋過新興市場的共同漲勢。政府提出將赤字由2026年占 GDP 5.4%降至2027年的4.9%，並在2029年達到區域3%上限後，其2033年美元債上漲0.5美分至53.73。價格反應肯定的是調整方向，而不是債務問題已經解決：先前未揭露負債使 IMF 在2024年暫停$18億計畫，基金組織仍要求由國際民間機構執行審計、完成積欠款項檢查並強化預算承諾控管。由於公共部門債務其後被估計在2024年底達 GDP 的132%，所有財政目標都仍取決於起始債務存量能否獲得驗證，以及市場准入能否維持。

黎巴嫩則呈現同一差異的回收價值面向。違約主權債交易價格約為面值的25美分，較戰前高點低約5美分；此前債券自2024年9月至2026年3月曾上漲432%。黎巴嫩自2020年3月對約$310億債務違約後未再付款，銀行體系損失估計接近$800億。原先認為債權人減記幅度可能不超過70%的預期，因此容易受到戰爭損害再起、銀行重整延誤，以及主權、央行、商業銀行與存款人之間損失分配尚未解決的衝擊。債券從困境價位大幅反彈，不代表回收價值已經確定。

美元走強加重了這些主權差異。Bloomberg美元指數6月已上漲約2%，今年以來上漲1.6%，投資人關注焦點由能源轉向 Warsh 主導下的 Fed 反應函數。年底前再升值5%及第3季再漲約2%的民間預測只是情境，並非已觀察到的市場數據，但足以說明美元借款人所面臨的風險方向。若美國政策維持限制性，而商品價格降溫降低當地通膨，新興市場央行即使取得國內政策空間，也可能因貨幣貶值與外債償付成本上升而無法運用。

中國在收盤後發布的數據，為下一交易日增添企業與主權兩面的背景。1月至5月，大型工業企業利潤較1年前成長18.8%，5月則成長21.1%，有利於應稅所得與企業現金創造。應收帳款增加7.7%，製成品庫存增加8.8%，資產負債率小幅升至58.2%。獲利轉強降低近期信用壓力，但現金轉換放慢與槓桿上升，使營運資金需求持續存在。因此，新興市場獲得的喘息仍因國而異：能源價格走低有所助益，但美元融資條件、擔保品設計及國內資產負債表品質，決定哪些國家能將其轉化為持久的市場准入。

## 跨資產

股票市場帶來的是集中型避險動能，而非廣泛的衰退訊號。S&P 500下跌0.0472%至7,354.02，Nasdaq 100下跌1.0940%，費城半導體指數則下跌5.2888%。亞洲科技股弱勢先蔓延至南韓，Kospi下跌5.8%，之後波及美國晶片股。估值疑慮、大型 AI 公司上市可能延後，以及市場對超大規模雲端業者支出報酬的質疑，推動了這波輪動。整體指數持平顯示衝擊侷限於特定產業，但其聚焦資本支出，使之與成長、通膨及信用供給直接相連。

這波賣壓也顯示，廣泛的新興市場曝險已變得高度集中。MSCI新興市場指數週五下跌2.8%、單週下跌4.4%；新興市場 ETF 的中位數約有30%半導體曝險，南韓與台灣合計權重近半。MSCI資料另顯示，4月底資訊科技占其新興市場指數比重已接近37%。對利率市場的意義並不是每個新興經濟體都承受相同晶片衝擊，而是基準指數與被動資金流出，可能把 AI 重新定價傳導至主權基本面、商品部位及政策週期截然不同的國家。

波動率使避險解讀更為複雜。即使半導體股遭到賣壓，VIX仍下跌2.5410%至18.41，單週則仍上漲12.2561%。投資人在收盤時為科技股持續分化定價，卻未付費防範不斷升高的全系統衝擊。這種型態符合美國公債的溫和漲勢：殖利率曲線腹部獲得部分避險需求，但波動率未飆升、整體股市亦未崩跌，使長端無法獲得更強的成長恐慌型買盤。

商品市場在通膨降溫與避險之間分化。WTI與Brent大幅下跌，進一步壓低損益兩平通膨率，並減輕能源消費者的進口成本負擔。黃金上漲1.1959%至$4,078.70，但單週仍下跌3.4422%，單月下跌9.3703%。黃金單日上漲顯示存在部分避險需求，但較長期跌勢仍與實質殖利率偏高一致。油價所發出的更強訊號，是週四短暫中斷後，通膨衝擊再度消退。

美元在單月大幅上漲後，週五幾乎持平。美元在油價下跌之際仍具韌性，意味支撐美國貨幣條件的仍是相對政策，而不只是能源稀缺。因此，跨資產強化了利率市場的核心分歧：集中的 AI 壓力與原油走低有利於2年至10年期美國公債，但波動率溫和、整體股市穩定及美元偏高，不支持名目需求或融資崩跌。SpaceX在次級市場的利差擴大，則提供股票辯論傳導至固定收益市場最清楚的橋梁，將對 AI 報酬的疑慮轉化為更高的長存續期間信用溢價。

## 市場前瞻意涵

6月29日至7月2日的美國經濟活動與勞動數據序列，將決定週五的殖利率曲線腹部漲勢能否承受需求證據的考驗。達拉斯聯準銀行製造業調查於週一上午10:30 ET公布，週二接續發布JOLTS與消費者信心，週三公布ADP就業與ISM製造業，週四上午8:30則發布6月就業報告與首次申請失業救濟人數。若聘僱放緩、職缺減少、製造業價格走軟，而產出維持穩定，將延續實質殖利率跌勢，並確認5年期區段可在通膨壓力消退且未發生嚴重衰退的情況下上漲。就業與薪資成長強勁、職缺保持韌性，或工廠投入價格再次上升，則會推高2年至5年期政策利率路徑，挑戰市場對 Fed 預期升息次數的下修，並重新開啟 FOMC 後的緊縮動能。

歐洲通膨與央行溝通日程，從6月29日的西班牙6月初值CPI及Sintra ECB Forum開幕開始，接續6月30日的德國通膨與7月1日的歐元區初值HICP。若各國通膨普遍放緩，隨後整體與核心通膨同步走軟，尤其是發言者承認能源價格及家庭預期下降，將驗證週五把 ECB 額外緊縮幅度降至不足25個基點的定價。核心服務通膨僵固、食品價格壓力再起，或類似Schnabel週末警告的指引，將推高德國公債前端殖利率與歐元區通膨殖利率曲線。這種結果將挑戰僅靠能源走勢逆轉便能降低全球政策底部的看法，並可能透過相對利率及海外需求傳導至美國公債期限溢價。

季末美國公債融資將在7月2日交割及縮短交易時段達到高峰。週一將標售$920億的13週期國庫券及$790億的26週期國庫券，週二標售$800億的6週期國庫券，週三供應17週期國庫券，週四則在建議下午2:00 ET提早收盤前標售4週期及8週期國庫券；市場於7月3日休市。若終端買方需求廣泛、投標情況有序，且現金國庫券殖利率在交割期間維持穩定，將確認週五的國庫券漲勢反映持久流動性，並防止融資壓力污染殖利率曲線腹部。若交易商大量承接、交割轉折前後價格突然走低，或同時遇上強勁就業數據，將挑戰融資有序的解讀；壓力將先透過國庫券及擔保融資傳導，之後才會威脅假期清淡市場中的附息債券流動性。

overall_summary.headline: 油價走低提振美國公債殖利率曲線腹部，長端阻力仍存
overall_summary.session_narrative: 2年至10年期美國公債上漲，以5年期債券領漲，長債則走弱。油價下跌、家庭預期降溫及模型估算的趨勢通膨下降，帶動實質殖利率與通膨補償同步走低，而科技股跌勢集中也增添避險需求。成長穩定、價格壓力廣泛、Fed 內部觀點分歧，以及財政與買方基礎疑慮，限制漲勢向殖利率曲線腹部以外延伸。
overall_summary.summary: 紐約收盤呈現由中期美國公債領軍的有序漲勢，能源價格走低與通膨預期降溫，減輕了近期政策壓力。成長韌性、Fed的警戒態度及長存續期間供給風險，使此波走勢未演變為全面的鴿派重新定價。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤走勢有利於政策敏感型存續期間獲得喘息，但尚未確認長期利率體制已轉低。
section_summaries.next_check: 漲勢能否持續，取決於勞動市場強勁與廣泛通膨是否讓 Fed 維持偏向抑制的立場。
section_summaries.summary: 油價走低與通膨預期降溫，降低了近期政策的迫切性，而科技股跌勢集中則增添對中期美國公債的避險需求。成長韌性、廣泛的通膨壓力、Fed的警戒態度及長端財政疑慮限制了走勢，使市場呈現由殖利率曲線腹部領軍的有序漲勢，而非全面的鴿派重新定價。

section_summaries.market_read: 殖利率曲線反映政策與成長交會處獲得喘息，但期限溢價仍未消除。
section_summaries.next_check: 就業、薪資及工廠價格必須顯示前端升息風險能否持續消退。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 5年期美國公債領漲，實質殖利率與通膨補償同步回落，而長債仍保留對通膨持續性、供給與買方不確定性的補償。成長穩定及 Fed 內部鷹派少數降低了市場對快速緊縮的預期，但並未排除進一步抑制的風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 初級市場承載能力仍充裕，但次級市場定價要求長期風險提供更多補償。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察最初的訂單簿支撐消退後，新發科技債能否順利完成定價。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用市場整體平靜，但發行後表現分化；儘管配售需求極為強勁，SpaceX 長債利差仍然擴大。大型科技企業的融資需求使市場准入維持暢通，卻迫使市場更嚴格區分期限與發行人，調整壓力由長存續期間及結構較弱的債權承擔。

section_summaries.market_read: 海外能源壓力緩解支撐美國公債殖利率曲線腹部，財政盤算則托住期限溢價。
section_summaries.next_check: 歐洲通膨的廣度與主權債發行必須證實全球政策底部正在下移。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 全球存續期間受惠於能源壓力下降，但各地政策與財政限制使漲勢無法一致。歐洲仍有通膨底部，加拿大與法國使主權供給疑慮持續存在；英國零售戰爭債券提案則顯示，擴大買方基礎仍無法消除國防借款及其期限溢價成本。

section_summaries.market_read: 新興市場獲得的喘息仍因國而異，因為美元流動性依舊主導各地從能源價格獲得的利益。
section_summaries.next_check: 監測貨幣走弱或國內殖利率上升是否觸發擔保品及保證金壓力。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 新興市場債近乎持平，能源價格走低帶來選擇性喘息，但美元偏高仍維持外部融資壓力。奈及利亞把再融資風險轉入擔保品與準備金；塞內加爾的赤字計畫仍取決於債務驗證與 IMF 條件；黎巴嫩的困境債反彈則仍受制於尚未分配的$800億銀行體系損失。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產訊號顯示的是產業壓力與通膨降溫帶來的喘息，而非全系統需求斷裂。
section_summaries.next_check: 關鍵考驗在於科技股弱勢是否擴散成波動率上升及更廣泛的融資壓力。
section_summaries.summary: 原油走低及集中的半導體賣壓支撐美國公債殖利率曲線腹部，但整體股市穩定、波動率下降，使長端未獲得衰退型買盤。半導體及南韓、台灣的高權重也顯示，AI 重新定價可透過新興市場基準指數傳導至其他國家。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The June 26 Treasury close extended the week’s duration rally from 2 through 10 years, yet the long bond’s loss turned the move into a more discriminating verdict on inflation relief. The 5-year yield fell 3 basis points to 4.12%, whereas the 30-year rose 1 basis point to 4.87%. Cheaper oil, cooler household inflation expectations and a lower New York Fed trend-inflation estimate reduced the need for an imminent policy response. A concentrated technology selloff added demand for intermediate Treasury protection. The curve still refused to endorse an unrestricted duration rally because resilient growth, broad price pressure and a divided Fed kept the terminal policy path uncertain, while fiscal and buyer-base concerns remained concentrated beyond 10 years.

Friday therefore restored the disinflation channel that had driven the June 24 rally after the prior close’s oil rebound briefly reopened the energy tail. Brent fell 4.3449% to $71.99, and both real yield and inflation compensation declined at 10 years. The important change was not a return to a dovish regime. Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari backed 1 increase this year because inflation had spread beyond energy, and the New York Fed’s growth nowcast remained stable. The close expressed less urgency around timing, especially in the belly, without rejecting the possibility of additional restraint. That combination explains why 5-year notes outperformed the front and long ends and why 2s30s steepened even as most nominal yields fell.

Credit and cross assets made the same distinction between relief and confidence. The broad equity index was nearly flat, but semiconductors fell sharply as doubts about AI valuations and capital spending moved from Asian shares into the US session. SpaceX’s $25 billion bond sale then accumulated about $360 million of losses relative to Treasuries by Friday afternoon, showing that exceptional primary capacity did not guarantee stable secondary absorption. Listed credit returns were quiet, so the strain remained concentrated in long-duration technology debt and lower-quality risk. The primary close thesis is consequently a controlled belly rally: energy disinflation and softer expectations lowered immediate policy pressure, while growth resilience, Fed vigilance, technology financing needs and long-end supply risk preserved a high clearing rate for duration and dollar-funded borrowers.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury performance weakened progressively beyond the belly. The 2-year yield fell 2 basis points to 4.07%, the 5-year declined 3 to 4.12%, and the 10-year eased 2 to 4.38%. The 30-year moved 1 basis point higher to 4.87%. Over the week, yields were lower by 12, 11, 8 and 3 basis points at those maturities, respectively. The daily and weekly patterns both place the strongest relief in maturities exposed to the next several policy cycles, while the long bond retained more compensation for inflation persistence, fiscal supply and an uncertain global buyer base.

Curve measures isolated that long-end resistance. The 2s10s spread held at 31 basis points, but 2s30s widened 3 basis points to 80. The 2s5s10s butterfly fell 2 basis points to -21, confirming 5-year outperformance against both neighboring wings. The 2s10s30s butterfly declined 3 basis points to -18 as the 10-year richened against the 2-year and 30-year average. This was neither the broad bull flattening of June 24 nor the simple front-end bull steepening that followed the first post-FOMC selloff. It was a rally centered on the policy-growth intersection, with the distant maturity left to carry the unresolved term premium.

Inflation-linked pricing gave the nominal rally an evenly divided composition at 10 years. The 10-year real yield fell 1 basis point to 2.18%, and the 10-year breakeven also declined 1 basis point to 2.20%. 5- and 7-year real yields eased 1 and 2 basis points to 1.91% and 2.03%, while the 30-year real yield was unchanged at 2.69%. Investors accepted slightly less real compensation in the belly as immediate hike anxiety receded, yet did not relax the real-rate hurdle at the far end. The drop in breakevens showed that cheaper energy was again entering nominal pricing after the previous session’s reversal.

Oil supplied the clearest reason for that change. WTI fell 3.7403% to $69.23, leaving it down 9.6214% over 1 week, and Brent’s weekly decline approached 10%. Continued tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz reduced the probability that Thursday’s vessel attack would re-establish a lasting physical shortage. The move removed part of the near-term fuel and freight impulse from inflation compensation. It did not settle broader price persistence, because the Fed’s preferred inflation measure had accelerated through May and several officials viewed the pressure as spread across categories.

Household expectations and modeled trend inflation nevertheless reduced the risk of immediate de-anchoring. The University of Michigan survey put 5- to 10-year inflation expectations at 3.3%, down from the earlier 3.4% reading. The New York Fed’s Multivariate Core Trend estimate declined to 3.4% in May from a downwardly revised 3.7% in April, with its 68% probability band spanning 3.0% to 3.9%. Services excluding housing remained the largest above-trend contributor, but its excess contribution fell to 0.83 percentage point from 0.91. Goods and housing contributions also moderated, making the improvement broader than a single energy component.

Growth evidence stopped that inflation relief from becoming an easing narrative. The New York Fed Staff Nowcast placed Q2 real GDP growth at 2.71%, little changed from 2.68% a week earlier. Its wide intervals counsel caution about precision, but the central estimate did not point to an abrupt demand break. Economists surveyed after the Fed meeting also raised their core PCE forecast for Q4 to 3.2% and their job-creation estimates, with the median path keeping the Fed on hold until June 2027. The mixture supports a longer pause more readily than an imminent cut, leaving rates responsive to inflation breadth and labor resilience.

Fed communication reinforced that asymmetry. Kashkari said Friday that widespread inflation led him to include 1 increase in his projection for this year. Nine of 19 policymakers had penciled in at least 1 increase after the June meeting. Market pricing still became less aggressive: by Friday, traders assigned roughly a 25% chance to 2 increases by year-end, down from almost 80% on Monday. The repricing reduced the probability of a rapid sequence, not the possibility of restraint. That is consistent with a lower 2-year yield and a richer belly alongside an unchanged long real yield.

The cash-bill curve showed no uniform quarter-end funding disturbance at the close. 4-week coupon-equivalent yield was unchanged at 3.68%, 6 weeks fell 2 basis points to 3.67%, and 8 weeks declined 1 to 3.73%. 13 weeks fell 3 basis points to 3.75%, while 17 and 26 weeks eased 1 basis point to 3.85% and 3.93%. The 52-week yield declined 3 basis points to 3.93%. Local differences remained, especially against month-earlier increases in the 13- through 52-week area, but Friday’s direction was broadly firmer as the market approached a heavy July 2 settlement date.

Weekend commentary, which came after the New York close, preserved the policy constraint for the next session without explaining Friday’s move. Richmond Fed President Tom Barkin said Sunday that inflation was too high, though falling gasoline and resistance from consumers to price increases offered tentative relief. He also linked some price pressure to AI infrastructure and described a modestly restrictive stance as reasonable. His emphasis on resilient spending and business price-setting echoed the close’s long-end caution: energy can cool quickly, but services, compensation and capital-intensive investment determine whether disinflation becomes durable.

The rates outcome therefore continued the week’s rally while changing its internal message again. June 24 had broadened energy relief into lower real yields, and June 25 had reintroduced breakeven risk when crude rebounded. Friday restored declines in both components and rewarded the 5-year sector most. The 30-year selloff, stable growth nowcast and hawkish Fed minority prevented that improvement from becoming a verdict that restrictive policy is finished. The belly carried the best balance of fading shock inflation and future slowdown risk; the long end remained the repository for fiscal, supply and persistence uncertainty.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Broad listed credit returns were subdued. LQD was unchanged at $109.50, while HYG and JNK each declined about 0.06%. Their 1-week changes remained modest: LQD gained 0.3942%, HYG lost 0.2250% and JNK fell 0.1764%. The aggregate tape showed neither a generalized financing seizure nor a strong search for lower-quality risk. Treasury gains supported higher-grade duration, while the technology selloff and still-high real-rate structure kept speculative-grade participation cautious.

SpaceX provided direct evidence of post-issuance strain. A dealer quoted the company’s 2056 bonds as much as 28 basis points wider than their issue spread of 175 basis points over Treasuries. The longest maturities erased the tightening achieved during syndication, after demand had once approached $90 billion. Losses on the $25 billion offering grew from roughly $305 million relative to Treasuries late Thursday to about $360 million by Friday afternoon. 5-year notes had attracted the strongest demand, whereas the 20- and 30-year tranches suffered the largest drop-off and subsequent weakness.

The deterioration changed the meaning of the week’s successful placement. Investors had demonstrated capacity to fund a very large investment-grade borrower, yet the secondary market exposed how much of the orderbook may have depended on fast-money demand and an expectation of an immediate trading gain. Years of anticipated negative cash flow, a concentrated governance profile and an upsized transaction made long-dated risk difficult to anchor. Stable SpaceX shares did not repair the bonds because creditors face fixed upside and decades of capital-spending and obsolescence exposure.

Technology credit showed a broader, milder version of that pressure. US high-grade tech risk premiums widened to 0.79 percentage point as of Thursday from 0.74 at the end of May. Alphabet bonds softened after the company announced an $85 billion equity sale, even though new equity ordinarily increases the creditor cushion. The market read the capital raise partly as a signal that AI expenditure and future borrowing could be larger than assumed. Equity issuance improved near-term liquidity while also advertising the scale of the funding cycle.

The supply backdrop explains the sensitivity. US high-grade issuance had reached $175 billion by Tuesday, already a June record. Estimates cited during the weekend placed AI and data-center spending through 2030 at $5.5 trillion and potential high-grade data-center financing over the next 5 years at $2.1 trillion. Those projections are not completed issuance, but they frame why buyers demanded greater differentiation by maturity and issuer. Capital intensity can keep access open while steadily raising the spread required to absorb another dollar of long-duration technology debt.

Johannesburg demonstrated a different form of creditor prioritization. The city repaid a 1.44 billion rand, or $88 million, bond carrying an 11.455% coupon, completing redemption of its 30 billion rand domestic medium-term-note program. The payment protected bondholders even as the finance minister threatened to withhold 8 billion rand of state funding over a disputed 10.3 billion rand wage agreement. Planned future infrastructure bonds would therefore approach the market after a successful maturity payment but amid severe operating and intergovernmental-finance stress.

The credit close was liquid in aggregate and punitive where duration, leverage or governance made valuation uncertain. Quiet listed-fund returns did not offset SpaceX’s direct spread widening, nor did the initial orderbook settle the borrower’s long-run clearing cost. Technology companies could still raise debt and equity at exceptional scale, and Johannesburg met a contractual payment despite fiscal strain. The common thread was access accompanied by sharper price discovery after funding arrived, with the longest and structurally weakest claims absorbing the greatest adjustment.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

European rates shared the energy relief but retained a policy floor. Traders reduced the amount of additional ECB tightening priced by the end of 2026 to less than a quarter point, after a full increase had been embedded 2 weeks earlier. The deposit rate stood at 2.25%. German 10-year yields fell to 2.85%, their lowest since March 10, and the euro-area 2-year inflation swap moved back below 2.2% from more than 3% in April. Sliding oil allowed duration to recover without signaling that the inflation overshoot had disappeared.

The ECB’s consumer survey clarified the split. 1-year inflation expectations fell to 3.5% in May from 4.0%, even before the US-Iran agreement. 3- and 5-year expectations remained elevated at 2.9% and 2.4%. Households also expected a 1.7% contraction in output over the next year, less severe than the 2.2% decline anticipated in April, while expected unemployment edged up to 11.3%. Near-term energy fear eased, but longer expectations and weak household growth perceptions left policymakers balancing a still-high inflation path against fragile demand.

Isabel Schnabel’s Saturday presentation arrived after the Treasury close and set a hawkish marker for the coming week. She warned of upside risks across food, goods and services, argued that an energy shock could broaden, and said more rate increases were expected to return inflation to 2%. She also noted no sign of wage pressure and acknowledged that the peace agreement made adverse outcomes less likely. The later remarks did not cause Friday’s Bund or Treasury rally; they underscored why the decline in market-implied tightening remains conditional on national and euro-area inflation releases.

Fiscal information added a separate duration channel. Canada’s Q1 general-government deficit widened to C$16.4 billion, or 2.1% of GDP, from 1.9% a year earlier. Headline net debt declined to C$538.1 billion and 16.5% of GDP, but debt excluding social-security funds increased 4.3% to C$1.49 trillion. The figures combine a better consolidated balance-sheet ratio with a larger current shortfall and rising debt outside funded social-security assets. Canadian duration therefore faces a less benign supply signal than the headline net-debt decline alone suggests.

France’s state auditor sharpened the longer-term European supply concern in a Saturday account. It said the government lacked a credible spending path to reduce the deficit from 5.1% of output last year to 3% by 2029. Because that assessment arrived after the US close, it belongs to the next global-duration evaluation. Its transmission runs through expected sovereign issuance, French risk premium and the fiscal latitude available before the 2027 presidential election, all of which can keep core and semi-core European term premium elevated even when energy inflation falls.

Britain added a more direct weekend debate over the form of sovereign financing. The rise of Andy Burnham revived proposals for retail “war bonds” to help fund higher defense spending, potentially by directing part of the roughly £70 billion that households place in ISAs each year toward government paper. Supporters argued that a tax-advantaged defense bond could raise another £20 billion; critics, including the outgoing prime minister, stressed that it would still be borrowing rather than free fiscal capacity. For gilt investors, the label matters less than the channel: a dedicated retail instrument could broaden the buyer base, but it would not remove the debt stock, interest cost or fiscal-rule trade-off created by additional defense outlays.

Asian activity did not support a synchronized global slowdown. Singapore’s May manufacturing output rose 13.0% from a year earlier, led by 35.8% growth in electronics and 32.2% in precision engineering, though total production fell 0.7% on a seasonally adjusted monthly basis. Biomedical output contracted 24.2%, chemicals 11.5% and transport engineering 5.0%. The narrow AI and semiconductor-equipment impulse supports regional demand and capital spending, yet the industry dispersion limits how strongly that growth signal should lift global real yields.

Global duration consequently benefited from a common fall in energy pressure but faced different local constraints. Europe paired lower near-term expectations with still-elevated medium-term gauges and hawkish ECB communication. Canada and France kept fiscal supply in view. Singapore showed strong AI-linked output without broad industrial breadth. These channels help explain the US curve’s maturity split: foreign inflation relief supported the Treasury belly, while fiscal arithmetic, local sovereign alternatives and persistent policy uncertainty maintained the higher clearing hurdle at the long end.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt was nearly unchanged at the close. EMB slipped 0.0207% to $96.54, even as the Dollar Index eased 0.0690% to 101.36. The dollar was still 0.5057% higher over 1 week and 2.2083% over 1 month, so Friday’s small decline did not remove the external financing constraint created by the Fed’s hawkish shift. Lower oil helped importers and local inflation paths, but countries dependent on foreign inflows remained exposed to US rates and the accumulated strength of the greenback.

Nigeria converted a complex liability structure into immediate cash. The sovereign drew about $1.5 billion from a $5 billion total-return swap with First Abu Dhabi Bank during the preceding weeks. It pledged naira securities equal to 133.3% of the borrowing as collateral. The 1st tranche carries a spread of 395 basis points over SOFR, rising to 400 basis points thereafter, and the funds are intended to refinance expensive debt and finance the budget deficit. External debt stood at $51.9 billion at the end of December.

The swap improves near-term liquidity while creating a contingent dollar claim. Fitch warned that margin calls payable in dollars could intensify foreign-exchange pressure if domestic yields rise or the naira weakens; the IMF also identified possible constraints on monetary and exchange-rate policy. Similar structures had been used by Angola and Senegal. Nigeria therefore obtained financing outside a conventional bond sale, but the overcollateralization and cross-currency margin mechanism transfer part of the refinancing risk into reserves, local rates and currency management.

The wider EM sensitivity had shifted from oil toward the Fed. The 30-day correlation between US 5-year yields and comparable Latin American rates rose to 0.49 from 0.10 at the end of February, with the Mexico relationship above 0.8 in June. Lower crude grants oil importers more inflation room, yet a stronger dollar makes central banks cautious about signaling easing. Turkey and Colombia stand out where reliance on foreign capital can transmit a hawkish US path quickly into local yields and currencies. Friday’s modest EMB move masked that growing macro linkage.

Senegal showed why country-specific fiscal credibility can dominate the common EM rally. Its 2033 dollar bonds rose 0.5 cent to 53.73 after the government set out a path to narrow the deficit to 4.9% of GDP in 2027 from 5.4% in 2026 and to reach the regional 3% ceiling in 2029. The price response rewarded a direction of travel, not closure of the debt problem: previously undisclosed liabilities had caused the IMF to suspend a $1.8 billion program in 2024, and the Fund still called for a private international audit, completion of the arrears review and stronger commitment controls. With public-sector debt later estimated at 132% of GDP at end-2024, every fiscal target remains contingent on the verified starting stock and continued market access.

Lebanon exposed the recovery-value side of the same distinction. Defaulted sovereign bonds traded near 25 cents on the dollar, about 5 cents below their prewar high, after having risen 432% between September 2024 and March 2026. The state has made no payment since defaulting on roughly $31 billion in March 2020, while the banking-system loss has been estimated near $80 billion. Expectations that creditor haircuts might be capped around 70% were therefore vulnerable to renewed war damage, delayed bank restructuring and the unresolved division of losses among the sovereign, central bank, commercial banks and depositors. A large rally from distressed levels did not make the recovery math settled.

A stronger dollar compounded those sovereign distinctions. The Bloomberg dollar gauge had gained about 2% in June and 1.6% for the year as investors shifted attention from energy toward the Warsh Fed’s reaction function. Private forecasts for a further 5% advance by year-end and roughly 2% in the third quarter were scenarios rather than observed market rows, but they illustrated the direction of risk facing dollar borrowers. If US policy stays restrictive while commodity relief lowers local inflation, EM central banks may gain domestic room yet still be unable to use it without weakening currencies and raising external debt-service costs.

A post-close Chinese release added a two-sided corporate-sovereign backdrop for the next session. Profits at large industrial companies rose 18.8% from a year earlier in January through May and 21.1% in May, supporting taxable income and corporate cash generation. Receivables increased 7.7%, finished-goods inventories 8.8%, and the asset-liability ratio edged up to 58.2%. Stronger earnings reduce immediate credit pressure, but slower cash conversion and higher leverage preserve working-capital demand. EM relief therefore remains country-specific: cheaper energy helps, while dollar funding terms, collateral design and domestic balance-sheet quality determine who can translate it into durable access.

## Cross asset

Equities generated a concentrated safety impulse rather than a broad recession signal. The S&P 500 slipped 0.0472% to 7,354.02, while the Nasdaq 100 fell 1.0940% and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index dropped 5.2888%. Asian technology weakness had moved through South Korea, where the Kospi lost 5.8%, before reaching US chip shares. Valuation concerns, possible delays to a major AI listing and questions about the returns on hyperscaler spending drove the rotation. The flat broad index showed that the shock was sectoral, but its capital-spending focus linked it directly to growth, inflation and credit supply.

The selloff also revealed how concentrated broad EM exposure had become. The MSCI Emerging Markets Index fell 2.8% on Friday and 4.4% over the week, while the median EM exchange-traded fund carried roughly 30% semiconductor exposure and almost half its weight in South Korea and Taiwan. MSCI data separately put information technology near 37% of its EM index by late April. The rates implication is not that every emerging economy shared the chip shock; it is that benchmark and passive outflows can transmit an AI repricing across countries whose sovereign fundamentals, commodity positions and policy cycles differ sharply.

Volatility complicated the haven interpretation. The VIX declined 2.5410% to 18.41 even as semiconductors sold off, and it remained 12.2561% higher over 1 week. Investors were marking persistent technology dispersion without paying for an escalating system-wide shock at the close. That pattern fits the modest Treasury rally: the belly received some protection demand, while the absence of a volatility surge or broad equity collapse denied the long end a stronger growth-scare bid.

Commodities split between disinflation and hedging. WTI and Brent fell sharply, reinforcing lower breakevens and reducing the imported-cost burden for energy consumers. Gold rose 1.1959% to $4,078.70, though it remained down 3.4422% over 1 week and 9.3703% over 1 month. The daily gold gain registered some demand for protection, but the longer decline remained consistent with elevated real yields. Oil’s much larger signal was the renewed retreat in the inflation shock after Thursday’s interruption.

The dollar was nearly unchanged on Friday after a strong monthly advance. Its resilience alongside falling oil means relative policy, not energy scarcity alone, continued to support US currency conditions. Cross assets therefore reinforced the core rates split: concentrated AI stress and cheaper crude favored 2- to 10-year Treasuries, while subdued volatility, stable broad equities and an elevated dollar argued against a collapse in nominal demand or funding. SpaceX’s secondary widening supplied the clearest bridge from the equity debate into fixed income, converting doubts about AI returns into a higher long-duration credit premium.

## Forward Market Significance

The US activity and labor sequence from June 29 through July 2 determines whether Friday’s belly rally can survive evidence on demand. The Dallas Fed manufacturing survey arrives Monday at 10:30 a.m. ET, followed Tuesday by JOLTS and consumer confidence, Wednesday by ADP employment and ISM manufacturing, and Thursday at 8:30 a.m. by June payrolls and initial claims. Slower hiring, fewer vacancies and softer manufacturing prices alongside stable output would extend the decline in real yields and confirm that the 5-year sector can rally as inflation pressure fades without a sharp recession. Strong payroll and wage growth, resilient vacancies or another rise in factory input prices would lift the 2- to 5-year policy path, challenge the reduction in expected Fed increases and reopen the post-FOMC tightening impulse.

European inflation and central-bank communication run from Spain’s June flash CPI and the opening of the ECB Forum in Sintra on June 29 through German inflation on June 30 and euro-area flash HICP on July 1. A broad national slowdown followed by softer aggregate headline and core inflation, especially if speakers acknowledge the fall in energy and household expectations, would validate Friday’s reduction to less than a quarter point of additional ECB tightening. Sticky core services, renewed food pressure or guidance resembling Schnabel’s weekend warning would push front-end Bund yields and the euro-area inflation curve higher. That outcome would challenge the idea that the energy reversal alone can lower the global policy floor and could transmit into Treasury term premium through relative rates and foreign demand.

Quarter-end Treasury financing culminates in a July 2 settlement and shortened trading session. Monday brings $92 billion of 13-week and $79 billion of 26-week bills, Tuesday an $80 billion 6-week sale, with 17-week supply Wednesday and 4- and 8-week auctions Thursday before the recommended 2:00 p.m. ET early close; the market is closed July 3. Broad end-buyer demand, orderly coverage and stable cash-bill yields through settlement would confirm that Friday’s bill rally reflected durable liquidity and would keep funding pressure from contaminating the belly. Dealer-heavy absorption, abrupt cheapening around the settlement turn or a collision with strong payroll data would challenge the orderly-funding interpretation, transmitting first through bills and secured financing before threatening coupon liquidity in a holiday-thinned market.

Resilient growth, broad inflation pressure, Fed vigilance and long-end fiscal concerns limited the move, leaving a controlled belly-led rally rather than a broad dovish repricing.

Softer demand with easing price pressure, broader European disinflation and orderly settlement demand would preserve relief; contrary outcomes would revive policy or funding pressure.

overall_summary.session_narrative: Treasuries rallied from 2 through 10 years, with 5-year notes leading while the long bond weakened. Falling oil, cooler household expectations and lower modeled trend inflation pulled both real yields and inflation compensation lower, while concentrated technology weakness added protection demand. Stable growth, broad price pressure, divided Fed views and fiscal and buyer-base concerns limited the rally beyond the belly.
overall_summary.summary: The NY close delivered a controlled rally led by intermediate Treasuries as cheaper energy and softer inflation expectations reduced immediate policy pressure. Resilient growth, Fed vigilance and long-duration supply risk kept the move from becoming a broad dovish repricing.

watch_events.market_implication: Strength in payrolls, wages or input prices would lift the two-to-5-year policy path.
watch_events.market_trigger: Slower hiring and softer factory prices with stable output would validate the belly rally.
watch_events.title: US activity and labor sequence
watch_events.watch_line: Track vacancies, hiring, wages, claims and manufacturing prices across the release sequence.

watch_events.market_implication: Sticky services or hawkish guidance would lift Bund front ends and Treasury term premium.
watch_events.market_trigger: Broad inflation slowing plus recognition of lower energy pressure would confirm reduced tightening.
watch_events.title: European inflation and ECB communication
watch_events.watch_line: Compare national and euro-area core inflation with the tone of ECB Forum guidance.

watch_events.market_implication: Dealer-heavy absorption or abrupt cheapening would threaten coupons in thin holiday trading.
watch_events.market_trigger: Orderly coverage and stable bill yields would confirm durable liquidity through settlement.
watch_events.title: Quarter-end Treasury financing
watch_events.watch_line: Watch end-buyer demand, auction coverage and cash-bill behavior into settlement.
